name: Redis — SET (single node, non-pipelined)
notes: redis-benchmark SET against one single-node Redis at the tool's default configuration (50 parallel clients, 3-byte payload, no pipelining, single key). Redis is single-threaded from the command-execution point of view, so SDA models it as a fixed-throughput M/M/1 store: the capacity that sets rho is the `throughput` cell (op/s), and the connection pool (1 slot) plus connectionHeldMs are kept EQUAL to it by construction (graph-read.ts: c/held = throughput) so the queueing TAIL is consistent with the ceiling. The scored metric is the throughput ceiling (client op/s at which the single-threaded store reaches rho = 1); the calibrated tunable is cache.redis:throughput — SDA's ESTIMATED 100,000 op/s default, corrected here to the measured value. The tunable is SHARED with the LPUSH entry — one capacity must reproduce BOTH simple O(1) commands, so the joint fit is over-determined and the residual is the honest command-to-command spread; the leave-one-out (fit on LPUSH, predict SET) is a genuine out-of-sample test. HONEST CAVEAT: Redis throughput is strongly configuration-dependent — redis.io's own examples span ~72,000 op/s (SET over a 100k-key random keyspace) to ~180,000+ (single key), and exceed 1,500,000 with -P 16 pipelining. This entry calibrates the model to ONE documented non-pipelined default config; the config-sensitivity (payload size, key-space locality, pipelining) and the loopback RTT that dominates the measured sub-0.15 ms p50 are structural factors SDA does not model (doc section 9) — so this validates the CEILING, not the tail.

sources.note: Official redis-benchmark documentation. `redis-benchmark -t set,lpush -n 100000 -q` reports `SET: 180180.17 requests per second, p50=0.143 msec`. Default config: 50 parallel clients (-c), 3-byte payload (-d), no pipelining (-P 1), single instance.
Redis FAQ: 'Redis is, mostly, a single-threaded server from the POV of command execution' — the sourced basis for the single-node M/M/1 model.

tunables.note: Redis single-node capacity ceiling (op/s) — the value nodeQueues reads for rho, with connectionPool/connectionHeldMs kept equal to it for the tail. Catalog default 100,000 op/s is an ESTIMATE (redis.io: 'single-threaded ~100k+ ops/s on one core'). SHARED with the LPUSH entry: one capacity must reproduce both the SET (180,180 op/s) and LPUSH (188,324 op/s) ceilings, so the fit is over-determined and the residual is the honest command-to-command spread one capacity value cannot remove.

groundTruth.note: `redis-benchmark -t set,lpush -n 100000 -q` -> `SET: 180180.17 requests per second, p50=0.143 msec` (default non-pipelined config). SDA's out-of-box estimate (100,000 op/s) UNDER-predicts by ~44%; the fit corrects the single-node capacity to the measured ceiling. (The measured p50 of 0.143 ms is dominated by loopback RTT + protocol, not the queueing service time SDA models, so it is not scored — the ceiling is.)
